title: Find the missing number (add and subtract within 1000)
description: Practice solving problems like "___ - 445 = 227" where you find the missing value in an addition or subtraction equation.
topic_path: Addition and subtraction missing value problems
Find the missing number (add and subtract within 1000)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number makes this equation true?** 

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] ${}+261=630$
hints:
$\goldE{?}+261=630$

![A number line with 2 points labeled. An arrow from point 261 to point 630 is labeled, plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eab97058966109151262a757c326858480c97bed)

Let's start by adding hundreds to ${261}$ until we get as close to  ${630}$ as possible without going over ${630}$.

$\begin{align}
{261} +\purpleD{100}=361\\\\
{361} +\purpleD{100}=461\\\\
{461} +\purpleD{100}=561
\end{align}$

If we add $\purpleD{3 \text{ hundreds}}$, or $\purpleD{300}$, we reach $561$. We cannot add anymore hundreds without going over ${630}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 261 to a new point 561 labeled, "plus 300"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d7d3e4657a388913f4a40eafdcf1ddf9ed2fa251)

Next, let's add tens to $561$ until we get as close to  ${630}$ as possible without going over ${630}$.

$\begin{align}
561 +\blueD{10}=571\\\\
{571} +\blueD{10}=581\\\\
{581} +\blueD{10}=591\\\\
{591} +\blueD{10}=601\\\\
{601} +\blueD{10}=611\\\\
{611} +\blueD{10}=621
\end{align}$

If we add $\blueD{6 \text{ tens}}$, or $\blueD{60}$, we reach $621$. We cannot add anymore tens without going over ${630}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 561 to a new point 621 labeled, "plus 60"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c1ed674b56440045ffbd657b21821d0d15feff27)

Finally, how many ones should we add to  $621$ to get to  ${630}?$

$621+\greenD{9}={630}$

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 621 to point 630 labeled, "plus 9"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5cd4ab050e5d1c77869d47bac3ab638c81b91d1e)

We added $\purpleD{3 \text{ hundreds}}$, $\blueD{6 \text{ tens}}$, and $\greenD{9\text{ ones}}$ to ${261}$ to get to ${630}$.

$\purpleD{300}+\blueD{60}+\greenD{9}=\goldE{369}$

![The number line with all of the arrows shown. The original arrow from point 261 to point 630 is labeled, "plus 369." The arrows spans the arrows labeled "plus 300," "plus 60," and "plus 9."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/81a94cf6b46963e8e44eab6f040c261c10e76abb)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number makes this equation true?** 

$872 + {}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] ${} = 981$
hints:
$872 + {\goldE{?}} = 981$

![A number line with 2 points labeled. An arrow from point 872 to point 981 is labeled, plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b53d60cd4c379946e4bdcbcc16882fe01b8d6beb)

Let's start by adding hundreds to ${872}$ until we get as close to  ${981}$ as possible without going over ${981}$.

$\begin{align}
{872} +\purpleD{100}=972
\end{align}$

If we add $\purpleD{1 \text{ hundred}}$, or $\purpleD{1 00}$, we reach $972$. We cannot add any more hundreds without going over ${981}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 872 to a new point 972 labeled, "plus 100"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/785b52c41739272b1db31bfb5691d477099f273c)

Next, let's add tens to $972$ until we get as close to  ${981}$ as possible without going over ${981}$.

We cannot add any tens without going over ${981}$.

Finally, how many ones should we add to  $972$ to get to  ${981}?$

$\begin{align}
972+\greenD{8} &=980\\\\
980+\greenD{1} &=981
\end{align}$

We add $\greenD{9\text{ ones}}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 972 to point 981 labeled, "plus 9"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7178d08963faf6124f74b0825c32aa1644bc925e)

We added $\purpleD{1 \text{ hundred}}$ and $\greenD{9\text{ ones}}$ to ${872}$ to get to ${981}$.

$\purpleD{1 00}+\greenD{9}=\goldE{109}$

![The number line with all of the arrows shown. The original arrow from point 872 to point 981 is labeled, "plus 109." The arrows spans the arrows labeled "plus 100" and "plus 9."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7a22567bef24e55548e06f0f7dc1a2eaff034864)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number makes this equation true?** 

$822 = {}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] ${} + 589$
hints:
$822 ={\goldE{?}} + 589$

![A number line with 2 points labeled. An arrow from point 589 to point 822 is labeled, plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a2eedb6f46d12bcbbdd7126ba709776626f06186)

Let's start by adding hundreds to ${589}$ until we get as close to  ${822}$ as possible without going over ${822}$.

$\begin{align}
{589} +\purpleD{100}=689\\\\
{689} +\purpleD{100}= 789
\end{align}$

If we add $\purpleD{2 \text{ hundreds}}$, or $\purpleD{2 00}$, we reach $789$. We cannot add any more hundreds without going over ${822}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 589 to a new point 789 labeled, "plus 200"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a351efaf9326f874732631f4d7b67b663f076400)

Next, let's add tens to $789$ until we get as close to  ${822}$ as possible without going over ${822}$.

$\begin{align}
789 +\blueD{10}=799\\\\
{799} +\blueD{10}= 809\\\\
{809} +\blueD{10}= 819
\end{align}$

If we add $\blueD{3 \text{ tens}}$, or $\blueD{30}$, we reach $819$. We cannot add any more tens without going over ${822}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 789 to a new point 819 labeled, "plus 30"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/137615dae32f78a18e16bfc1f538b8c0fc939f80)

Finally, how many ones should we add to  $819$ to get to  ${822}?$

$\begin{align}
819+\greenD{1} &=820\\\\
820+\greenD{2} &=822
\end{align}$

We add $\greenD{3\text{ ones}}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 819 to point 822 labeled, "plus 3"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d1f036e4bdc3ccbf6d5f8bc61b529d4885029059)

We added $\purpleD{2 \text{ hundreds}}$, $\blueD{3 \text{ tens}}$, and $\greenD{3\text{ ones}}$ to ${589}$ to get to ${822}$.

$\purpleD{2 00}+\blueD{3 0}+\greenD{3}=\goldE{233}$

![The number line with all of the arrows shown. The original arrow from point 589 to point 822 is labeled, "plus 233." The arrows spans the arrows labeled "plus 200," "plus 30," and "plus 3."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5d51bdd9b7eb28dad098f1d169d4c956981fe375)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number makes this equation true?** 

$719 = 57 + {}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
$719 = 57 +{\goldE{?}}$

![A number line with 2 points labeled. An arrow from point 57 to point 719 is labeled, plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/425db097f24988af611ecaef719c0aea129bdc75)

Let's start by adding hundreds to ${57}$ until we get as close to  ${719}$ as possible without going over ${719}$.

$\begin{align}
{57} +\purpleD{100}=157\\\\
{157} +\purpleD{100}= 257\\\\
{257} +\purpleD{100}= 357\\\\
{357} +\purpleD{100}= 457\\\\
{457} +\purpleD{100}= 557\\\\
{557} +\purpleD{100}= 657
\end{align}$

If we add $\purpleD{6 \text{ hundreds}}$, or $\purpleD{6 00}$, we reach $657$. We cannot add any more hundreds without going over ${719}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 57 to a new point 657 labeled, "plus 600"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/48326579527370fd594d97f961beb601e95cbef4)

Next, let's add tens to $657$ until we get as close to  ${719}$ as possible without going over ${719}$.

$\begin{align}
657 +\blueD{10}=667\\\\
{667} +\blueD{10}= 677\\\\
{677} +\blueD{10}= 687\\\\
{687} +\blueD{10}= 697\\\\
{697} +\blueD{10}= 707\\\\
{707} +\blueD{10}= 717
\end{align}$

If we add $\blueD{6 \text{ tens}}$, or $\blueD{60}$, we reach $717$. We cannot add any more tens without going over ${719}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 657 to a new point 717 labeled, "plus 60"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/984c0c0638e29e1464f5ddb7e815927a26959f2f)

Finally, how many ones should we add to  $717$ to get to  ${719}?$

$717 +\greenD{2}={719}$

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 717 to point 719 labeled, "plus 2"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4532fb31d4934b519e62ea0b3494f6c6db198ec9)

We added $\purpleD{6 \text{ hundreds}}$, $\blueD{6 \text{ tens}}$, and $\greenD{2\text{ ones}}$ to ${57}$ to get to ${719}$.

$\purpleD{6 00}+\blueD{6 0}+\greenD{2}=\goldE{662}$

![The number line with all of the arrows shown. The original arrow from point 57 to point 719 is labeled, "plus 662." The arrows spans the arrows labeled "plus 600," "plus 60," and "plus 2."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a51417161c8606d039f1855551a3bd67e33063f5)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number makes this equation true?** 

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] ${} + 393 =706$
hints:
$\goldE{?}+ 393 = 706$

![A number line with 2 points labeled. An arrow from point 393 to point 706 is labeled, plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5c59ef9812c0af0117360d3d0b1cba6c9ac6d1c6)

Let's start by adding hundreds to ${393}$ until we get as close to  ${706}$ as possible without going over ${706}$.

$\begin{align}
{393} +\purpleD{100}=493\\\\
{493} +\purpleD{100}= 593\\\\
{593} +\purpleD{100}= 693
\end{align}$

If we add $\purpleD{3 \text{ hundreds}}$, or $\purpleD{3 00}$, we reach $693$. We cannot add any more hundreds without going over ${706}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 393 to a new point 693 labeled, "plus 300"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d65d1ff0d0c17242b798414a6665ece68616a6c6)

Next, let's add tens to $693$ until we get as close to  ${706}$ as possible without going over ${706}$.

$\begin{align}
693 +\blueD{10}=703
\end{align}$

If we add $\blueD{1 \text{ ten}}$, or $\blueD{10}$, we reach $703$. We cannot add any more tens without going over ${706}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 693 to a new point 703 labeled, "plus 10"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a7a8913a20912abfc24fe36b0e7bb161deda9b6f)

Finally, how many ones should we add to  $703$ to get to  ${706}?$

$703 +\greenD{3}={706}$

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 703 to point 706 labeled, "plus 3"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/235b99542739a4e2be90f4c3d64edaa570cc4d29)

We added $\purpleD{3 \text{ hundreds}}$, $\blueD{1 \text{ ten}}$, and $\greenD{3\text{ ones}}$ to ${393}$ to get to ${706}$.

$\purpleD{3 00}+\blueD{1 0}+\greenD{3}=\goldE{313}$

![The number line with all of the arrows shown. The original arrow from point 393 to point 706 is labeled, "plus 313." The arrows spans the arrows labeled "plus 300," "plus 10," and "plus 3."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/18c440a298109efb2e9ca18390db77cae1c42606)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number makes this equation true?** 

$428 + {}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] ${} = 993$
hints:
$428 +{\goldE{?}}= 993$

![A number line with 2 points labeled. An arrow from point 428 to point 993 is labeled, plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ac010379f8018e8ff9d68857e114fada75e278e3)

Let's start by adding hundreds to ${428}$ until we get as close to  ${993}$ as possible without going over ${993}$.

$\begin{align}
{428} +\purpleD{100}=528\\\\
{528} +\purpleD{100}= 628\\\\
{628} +\purpleD{100}= 728\\\\
{728} +\purpleD{100}= 828\\\\
{828} +\purpleD{100}= 928
\end{align}$

If we add $\purpleD{5 \text{ hundreds}}$, or $\purpleD{5 00}$, we reach $928$. We cannot add any more hundreds without going over ${993}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 428 to a new point 928 labeled, "plus 500"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d2aeb06d1e33abbf8d7175ffb2d875979ad8e9c0)

Next, let's add tens to $928$ until we get as close to  ${993}$ as possible without going over ${993}$.

$\begin{align}
928 +\blueD{10}=938\\\\
{938} +\blueD{10}= 948\\\\
{948} +\blueD{10}= 958\\\\
{958} +\blueD{10}= 968\\\\
{968} +\blueD{10}= 978\\\\
{978} +\blueD{10}= 988
\end{align}$

If we add $\blueD{6 \text{ tens}}$, or $\blueD{60}$, we reach $988$. We cannot add any more tens without going over ${993}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 928 to a new point 988 labeled, "plus 60"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2563d10b40ddf5de6c12792578832320e4753e73)

Finally, how many ones should we add to  $988$ to get to  ${993}?$

$\begin{align}
988+\greenD{2} &=990\\\\
990+\greenD{3} &=993
\end{align}$

We add $\greenD{5\text{ ones}}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 988 to point 993 labeled, "plus 5"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ce12548cac83cdcd49aac2d98c349b72a9790d21)

We added $\purpleD{5 \text{ hundreds}}$, $\blueD{6 \text{ tens}}$, and $\greenD{5\text{ ones}}$ to ${428}$ to get to ${993}$.

$\purpleD{5 00}+\blueD{6 0}+\greenD{5}=\goldE{565}$

![The number line with all of the arrows shown. The original arrow from point 428 to point 993 is labeled, "plus 565." The arrows spans the arrows labeled "plus 500," "plus 60," and "plus 5."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6a45ea17188a69f173deb1bf3ea4b7ee8f6b5577)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number makes this equation true?** 

$615 = {}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] ${} + 544$
hints:
$615 = \goldE{?}+ 544$

![A number line with 2 points labeled. An arrow from point 544 to point 615 is labeled, plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/524aa8728ca4790e55cad9cd1c938251714bcb68)

Let's start by adding hundreds to ${544}$ until we get as close to  ${615}$ as possible without going over ${615}$.

We cannot add any hundreds without going over ${615}$.

Next, let's add tens to $544$ until we get as close to  ${615}$ as possible without going over ${615}$.

$\begin{align}
544 +\blueD{10}=554\\\\
{554} +\blueD{10}= 564\\\\
{564} +\blueD{10}= 574\\\\
{574} +\blueD{10}= 584\\\\
{584} +\blueD{10}= 594\\\\
{594} +\blueD{10}= 604\\\\
{604} +\blueD{10}= 614
\end{align}$

If we add $\blueD{7 \text{ tens}}$, or $\blueD{70}$, we reach $614$. We cannot add any more tens without going over ${615}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 544 to a new point 614 labeled, "plus 70"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/96b9afaa6a0713483daaba310bcdd611801e1fd0)

Finally, how many ones should we add to  $614$ to get to  ${615}?$

$614 +\greenD{1}={615}$

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 614 to point 615 labeled, "plus 1"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/827022498e2c777a182541baa342fdcde66e1b14)

We added $\blueD{7 \text{ tens}}$ and $\greenD{1\text{ one}}$ to ${544}$ to get to ${615}$.

$\blueD{7 0}+\greenD{1}=\goldE{71}$

![The number line with all of the arrows shown. The original arrow from point 544 to point 615 is labeled, "plus 71." The arrows spans the arrows labeled, "plus 70," and "plus 1."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bf8b373105e2850fa43046a6ea39694e558eb89b)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number makes this equation true?** 

$557 = 106 + {}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
$557 = 106 +{\goldE{?}}$

![A number line with 2 points labeled. An arrow from point 106 to point 557 is labeled, plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6f15446867caa41609f07ddd16015d29158b5976)

Let's start by adding hundreds to ${106}$ until we get as close to  ${557}$ as possible without going over ${557}$.

$\begin{align}
{106} +\purpleD{100}=206\\\\
{206} +\purpleD{100}= 306\\\\
{306} +\purpleD{100}= 406\\\\
{406} +\purpleD{100}= 506
\end{align}$

If we add $\purpleD{4 \text{ hundreds}}$, or $\purpleD{4 00}$, we reach $506$. We cannot add any more hundreds without going over ${557}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 106 to a new point 506 labeled, "plus 400"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/edfec1409772c8168bdfd119f4eaf9513302716a)

Next, let's add tens to $506$ until we get as close to  ${557}$ as possible without going over ${557}$.

$\begin{align}
506 +\blueD{10}=516\\\\
{516} +\blueD{10}= 526\\\\
{526} +\blueD{10}= 536\\\\
{536} +\blueD{10}= 546\\\\
{546} +\blueD{10}= 556
\end{align}$

If we add $\blueD{5 \text{ tens}}$, or $\blueD{50}$, we reach $556$. We cannot add any more tens without going over ${557}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 506 to a new point 556 labeled, "plus 50"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/200874afee6b1596d7cfb521c4597b5e7d811329)

Finally, how many ones should we add to  $556$ to get to  ${557}?$

$556 +\greenD{1}={557}$

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 556 to point 557 labeled, "plus 1"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/15ade583635cb2075e796f49e6668b3f66a612fd)

We added $\purpleD{4 \text{ hundreds}}$, $\blueD{5 \text{ tens}}$, and $\greenD{1\text{ one}}$ to ${106}$ to get to ${557}$.

$\purpleD{4 00}+\blueD{5 0}+\greenD{1}=\goldE{451}$

![The number line with all of the arrows shown. The original arrow from point 106 to point 557 is labeled, "plus 451." The arrows spans the arrows labeled "plus 400," "plus 50," and "plus 1."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f2b5c0e0ce037b40968a2fc4c807ffe4c13e5dc4)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number makes this equation true?** 

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] ${} + 445 =864$
hints:
$\goldE{?}+ 445 = 864$

![A number line with 2 points labeled. An arrow from point 445 to point 864 is labeled, plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/782da27ad8795182b7f5ea316a9a02c3f53f469f)

Let's start by adding hundreds to ${445}$ until we get as close to  ${864}$ as possible without going over ${864}$.

$\begin{align}
{445} +\purpleD{100}=545\\\\
{545} +\purpleD{100}= 645\\\\
{645} +\purpleD{100}= 745\\\\
{745} +\purpleD{100}= 845
\end{align}$

If we add $\purpleD{4 \text{ hundreds}}$, or $\purpleD{4 00}$, we reach $845$. We cannot add any more hundreds without going over ${864}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 445 to a new point 845 labeled, "plus 400"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ea63701a8f3d42b93977d40a80005537a6d673b8)

Next, let's add tens to $845$ until we get as close to  ${864}$ as possible without going over ${864}$.

$\begin{align}
845 +\blueD{10}=855
\end{align}$

If we add $\blueD{1 \text{ ten}}$, or $\blueD{10}$, we reach $855$. We cannot add any more tens without going over ${864}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 845 to a new point 855 labeled, "plus 10"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/acc65e17b3390c5f833075372628b04bda1a83a0)

Finally, how many ones should we add to  $855$ to get to  ${864}?$

$\begin{align}
855+\greenD{5} &=860\\\\
860+\greenD{4} &=864
\end{align}$

We add $\greenD{9\text{ ones}}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 855 to point 864 labeled, "plus 9"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/aca28d33524479b8309475c27060df5d2881125d)

We added $\purpleD{4 \text{ hundreds}}$, $\blueD{1 \text{ ten}}$, and $\greenD{9\text{ ones}}$ to ${445}$ to get to ${864}$.

$\purpleD{4 00}+\blueD{1 0}+\greenD{9}=\goldE{419}$

![The number line with all of the arrows shown. The original arrow from point 445 to point 864 is labeled, "plus 419." The arrows spans the arrows labeled "plus 400," "plus 10," and "plus 9."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9dae6c86412cef81543a0b7b5e73f9459dd73f8b)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number makes this equation true?** 

$230 + {}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] ${} = 378$
hints:
$230 +{\goldE{?}}= 378$

![A number line with 2 points labeled. An arrow from point 230 to point 378 is labeled, plus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0bcb2b4913e797dddd4d58ecd037a4f7e0af1ec0)

Let's start by adding hundreds to ${230}$ until we get as close to  ${378}$ as possible without going over ${378}$.

$\begin{align}
{230} +\purpleD{100}=330
\end{align}$

If we add $\purpleD{1 \text{ hundred}}$, or $\purpleD{1 00}$, we reach $330$. We cannot add any more hundreds without going over ${378}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 230 to a new point 330 labeled, "plus 100"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b319ea8c8e3ec02eff5b6c7ce7e8ff006ce8f5f4)

Next, let's add tens to $330$ until we get as close to  ${378}$ as possible without going over ${378}$.

$\begin{align}
330 +\blueD{10}=340\\\\
{340} +\blueD{10}= 350\\\\
{350} +\blueD{10}= 360\\\\
{360} +\blueD{10}= 370
\end{align}$

If we add $\blueD{4 \text{ tens}}$, or $\blueD{40}$, we reach $370$. We cannot add any more tens without going over ${378}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 330 to a new point 370 labeled, "plus 40"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a7af956880a0f172eb7b93f01ece194ecc0af6ea)

Finally, how many ones should we add to  $370$ to get to  ${378}?$

$370 +\greenD{8}={378}$

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 370 to point 378 labeled, "plus 8"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b0fa98af23991d9a6c444ea7514b4f3d118be069)

We added $\purpleD{1 \text{ hundred}}$, $\blueD{4 \text{ tens}}$, and $\greenD{8\text{ ones}}$ to ${230}$ to get to ${378}$.

$\purpleD{1 00}+\blueD{4 0}+\greenD{8}=\goldE{148}$

![The number line with all of the arrows shown. The original arrow from point 230 to point 378 is labeled, "plus 148." The arrows spans the arrows labeled "plus 100," "plus 40," and "plus 8."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d81094d25599fb8fe125f629b7f4240e38393833)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number makes this equation true?**

 [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $- \, 435=210$
hints:
$\greenD{?}-\goldE{435}= \purpleD{210}$

![A number line with 2 points labeled. An arrow from point question mark to point 210 is labeled, minus 435.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2445f56b09e21af3fb655edec625ec04e28e85f9)

What number can we subtract $\goldE{435}$ from and be left with $\purpleD{210}?$

We can add $\goldE{435}$ to $\purpleD{210}$ to find the missing number.

$\begin{align}{\goldE{435}}&\\
\underline{+\purpleD{210}} &\\
\greenD{645}&\end{align}$

![The same number line as above, with the question mark replaced with 645 and an arrow from 210 to 645 labeled plus 435](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1cf750a0907f1557a97b8469bdc726629e825b89)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number makes this equation true?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] ${} - 47 = 772$
hints:
$\greenD{?}-\goldE{47} = \purpleD{772}$

![A number line with 2 points labeled. An arrow from point question mark back to point 772 is labeled, minus 47.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/aa3ef2290ebbe6e5aa3ca55a8eb85cd3cbf25934)

What number can we subtract $\goldE{47}$ from and be left with $\purpleD{772}?$

We can add $\goldE{47}$ to $\purpleD{772}$ to find the missing number.

$\begin{align}\goldE{47}&\\
\underline{+ \purpleD{772}} &\\
\greenD{819}&\end{align}$

![The same number line as above, with the question mark replaced with 819 and an arrow from 772 to 819 labeled plus 47](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6f14a3f92c50da918823f870976d9f905a868870)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number makes this equation true?**

$495 = 621 - $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
$495 = 621-\,?$

![A number line with 2 points labeled. An arrow from point 621 back to point 495 is labeled, minus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bdcd987755f620b9d6ca11cd190bf4bf5d4aef1b)

Let's start by subtracting hundreds from ${621}$ until we get as close to  ${495}$ as possible without going below ${495}$.

${621} -\purpleD{100}=521$

If we subtract $\purpleD{1 \text{ hundred}}$, or $\purpleD{100}$, we reach $521$. We cannot subtract any more hundreds without going below ${495}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 621 back to a new point 521 labeled, "minus 100"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/559f2608b2f800f7169f84ffdcccc034e88fe0e3)

Next, we can subtract tens from $521$ until we get as close to  ${495}$ as possible without going below ${495}$.

$\begin{align}
521 -\blueD{10}=511\\\\
511 - \blueD{10} = 501
\end{align}$

If we subtract $\blueD{2 \text{ tens}}$, or $\blueD{20}$, we reach $501$. We cannot subtract any more tens without going below ${495}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 521 to a new point 501 labeled, "minus 20"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b6b283d5acfc9d55ea40dc8ac17c1e58e4de6498)

Finally, how many ones should we subtract from $501$ to get to  ${495}?$

$\begin{align}
501-\greenD{1}={500}\\\\
500-\greenD{5}={495}
\end{align}$

If we subtract $\greenD{6\text{ ones}}$ from $501$, we reach ${495}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 501 back to point 495 labeled, "minus 6"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/491ac6d9b8b9bc66fa1995c40905d9ef29a8b16b)

We subtracted $\purpleD{1 \text{ hundred}}$, $\blueD{2 \text{ tens}}$, and $\greenD{6 \text{ ones}}$ from $\blueD{621}$ to get to $\greenD{495}$.

$\purpleD{100}+\blueD{20}+\greenD{6}=\goldE{126}$

![The number line with all of the arrows shown. The original arrow from point 621 back to point 495 is labeled, "minus 126." The arrows spans the arrows labeled "minus 100," "minus 20," and "minus 6."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4d82d5614f11a4402253a24827bdac6c4c0c1cdf)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number makes this equation true?**

$960 - {}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] ${} = 581$
hints:
$960 -{\goldE{?}}= 581$

![A number line with 2 points labeled. An arrow from point 960 back to point 581 is labeled, minus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/39a4776ff268f260f00a1c4cd15c48d87888e133)

Let's start by subtracting hundreds from ${960}$ until we get as close to  ${581}$ as possible without going below ${581}$.

$\begin{align}
{960} -\purpleD{100}=860\\\\
{860} -\purpleD{100}= 760\\\\
{760} -\purpleD{100}= 660
\end{align}$

If we subtract $\purpleD{3 \text{ hundreds}}$, or $\purpleD{3 00}$, we reach $660$. We cannot subtract any more hundreds without going below ${581}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 960 back to a new point 660 labeled, "minus 300"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b08ddf038a90675ae35aee0606341df3e9ebee81)

Next, let's subtract tens from $660$ until we get as close to  ${581}$ as possible without going below ${581}$.

$\begin{align}
660 -\blueD{10}=650\\\\
{650} -\blueD{10}= 640\\\\
{640} -\blueD{10}= 630\\\\
{630} -\blueD{10}= 620\\\\
{620} -\blueD{10}= 610\\\\
{610} -\blueD{10}= 600\\\\
{600} -\blueD{10}= 590
\end{align}$

If we subtract $\blueD{7 \text{ tens}}$, or $\blueD{70}$, we reach $590$. We cannot subtract any more tens without going below ${581}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 660 back to a new point 590 labeled, "minus 70"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fccff3e3a544e66df01de40c636fb18d71e7e7e5)

Finally, how many ones should we subtract from $590$ to get to  ${581}?$

$590 -\greenD{9}={581}$

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 590 back to point 581 labeled, "minus 9"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1386772c0dfa02a4e203cec1d3a2a67aa3f870fa)

We subtracted $\purpleD{3 \text{ hundreds}}$, $\blueD{7 \text{ tens}}$, and $\greenD{9\text{ ones}}$ from ${960}$ to get to ${581}$.

$\purpleD{3 00}+\blueD{7 0}+\greenD{9}=\goldE{379}$

![The number line with all of the arrows shown. The original arrow from point 960 to point 581 is labeled, "minus 379." The arrows spans the arrows labeled "minus 300," "minus 70," and "minus 9."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4114a7b4a5000695e0cc4f08bdc2a38cc38597f2)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number makes this equation true?**

$327 = 808 - {}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
$327 = 808 -{\goldE{?}}$

![A number line with 2 points labeled. An arrow from point 808 back to point 327 is labeled, minus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/472a4468a30db371de347ff74cfe5d97640768ed)

Let's start by subtracting hundreds from ${808}$ until we get as close to  ${327}$ as possible without going below ${327}$.

$\begin{align}
{808} -\purpleD{100}=708\\\\
{708} -\purpleD{100}= 608\\\\
{608} -\purpleD{100}= 508\\\\
{508} -\purpleD{100}= 408
\end{align}$

If we subtract $\purpleD{4 \text{ hundreds}}$, or $\purpleD{4 00}$, we reach $408$. We cannot subtract any more hundreds without going below ${327}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 808 back to a new point 408 labeled, "minus 400"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9695ca85fd43cacb5da43a5e9cd93d4a8b5ef434)

Next, let's subtract tens from $408$ until we get as close to  ${327}$ as possible without going below ${327}$.

$\begin{align}
408 -\blueD{10}=398\\\\
{398} -\blueD{10}= 388\\\\
{388} -\blueD{10}= 378\\\\
{378} -\blueD{10}= 368\\\\
{368} -\blueD{10}= 358\\\\
{358} -\blueD{10}= 348\\\\
{348} -\blueD{10}= 338\\\\
{338} -\blueD{10}= 328
\end{align}$

If we subtract $\blueD{8 \text{ tens}}$, or $\blueD{80}$, we reach $328$. We cannot subtract any more tens without going below ${327}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 408 back to a new point 328 labeled, "minus 80"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e42491da2fd9fb77ccf9e06d4b81939152c1d3ef)

Finally, how many ones should we subtract from $328$ to get to  ${327}?$

$328 -\greenD{1}={327}$

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 328 back to point 327 labeled, "minus 1"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4082df7fc16f82c08ccd2637785fb299aa55d54f)

We subtracted $\purpleD{4 \text{ hundreds}}$, $\blueD{8 \text{ tens}}$, and $\greenD{1\text{ one}}$ from ${808}$ to get to ${327}$.

$\purpleD{4 00}+\blueD{8 0}+\greenD{1}=\goldE{481}$

![The number line with all of the arrows shown. The original arrow from point 808 to point 327 is labeled, "minus 481." The arrows spans the arrows labeled "minus 400," "minus 80," and "minus 1."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3b5c562081d02e01228045ea5410c76ed7fbe7bc)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number makes this equation true?**

$596 - {}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] ${} = 49$
hints:
$596 -{\goldE{?}}= 49$

![A number line with 2 points labeled. An arrow from point 596 back to point 49 is labeled, minus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e094b337e8866b1d9125607590be1a4b5319079a)

Let's start by subtracting hundreds from ${596}$ until we get as close to  ${49}$ as possible without going below ${49}$.

$\begin{align}
{596} -\purpleD{100}=496\\\\
{496} -\purpleD{100}= 396\\\\
{396} -\purpleD{100}= 296\\\\
{296} -\purpleD{100}= 196\\\\
{196} -\purpleD{100}= 96
\end{align}$

If we subtract $\purpleD{5 \text{ hundreds}}$, or $\purpleD{5 00}$, we reach $96$. We cannot subtract any more hundreds without going below ${49}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 596 back to a new point 96 labeled, "minus 500"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fa68600469d271213ea69ae326b8d9b625b733fc)

Next, let's subtract tens from $96$ until we get as close to  ${49}$ as possible without going below ${49}$.

$\begin{align}
96 -\blueD{10}=86\\\\
{86} -\blueD{10}= 76\\\\
{76} -\blueD{10}= 66\\\\
{66} -\blueD{10}= 56
\end{align}$

If we subtract $\blueD{4 \text{ tens}}$, or $\blueD{40}$, we reach $56$. We cannot subtract any more tens without going below ${49}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 96 back to a new point 56 labeled, "minus 40"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1270ae4e604451728499d4d56b27ce0915bb829a)

Finally, how many ones should we subtract from $56$ to get to  ${49}?$

$\begin{align}
56-\greenD{6} &= 50\\\\
50-\greenD{1} &= 49
\end{align}$

We subtract $\greenD{7\text{ ones}}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 56 back to point 49 labeled, "minus 7"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/08a558a3af01df5e014230a115c439e0df0ca1be)

We subtracted $\purpleD{5 \text{ hundreds}}$, $\blueD{4 \text{ tens}}$, and $\greenD{7\text{ ones}}$ from ${596}$ to get to ${49}$.

$\purpleD{5 00}+\blueD{4 0}+\greenD{7}=\goldE{547}$

![The number line with all of the arrows shown. The original arrow from point 596 to point 49 is labeled, "minus 547." The arrows spans the arrows labeled "minus 500," "minus 40," and "minus 7."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/09a599e693d3ca33d811fde0ff277326bd491724)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What number makes this equation true?**

$148 = 754 - {}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
$148 = 754 -{\goldE{?}}$

![A number line with 2 points labeled. An arrow from point 754 back to point 148 is labeled, minus question mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0f622f93740f292bcbb643af131106685660a31d)

Let's start by subtracting hundreds from ${754}$ until we get as close to  ${148}$ as possible without going below ${148}$.

$\begin{align}
{754} -\purpleD{100}=654\\\\
{654} -\purpleD{100}= 554\\\\
{554} -\purpleD{100}= 454\\\\
{454} -\purpleD{100}= 354\\\\
{354} -\purpleD{100}= 254\\\\
{254} -\purpleD{100}= 154
\end{align}$

If we subtract $\purpleD{6 \text{ hundreds}}$, or $\purpleD{6 00}$, we reach $154$. We cannot subtract any more hundreds without going below ${148}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 754 back to a new point 154 labeled, "minus 600"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/69c16a6135a6bfaaec7d5cd97f2aa7b89f0ada09)

Next, let's subtract tens from $154$ until we get as close to  ${148}$ as possible without going below ${148}$.

We cannot subtract any tens without going below ${148}$.

Finally, how many ones should we subtract from $154$ to get to  ${148}?$

$\begin{align}
154-\greenD{4} &= 150\\\\
150-\greenD{2} &= 148
\end{align}$

We subtract $\greenD{6\text{ ones}}$.

![The same number line, with an arrow from point 154 back to point 148 labeled, "minus 6"](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/98ed42851444e91a954e7fde5527fe2f656cc698)

We subtracted $\purpleD{6 \text{ hundreds}}$ and $\greenD{6\text{ ones}}$ from ${754}$ to get to ${148}$.

$\purpleD{6 00}+\greenD{6}=\goldE{606}$

![The number line with all of the arrows shown. The original arrow from point 754 to point 148 is labeled, "minus 606." The arrows spans the arrows labeled "minus 600," "minus 00," and "minus 6."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0331ea743a254165a5bd98d019a230438658c11b)
